feed.title: SAP Community - Sheena M K
feed.subtitle: Sheena M K in SAP Community

entry.title: 🟢 How to Extend the different SAP S/4HANA Editions Effectively and Clean Core Compliant
entry.content:
This session will explain how to extend the SAP S/4HANA editions effectively following the clean core guidance. This includes the 3-tier extensibility model, ABAP Cloud, CAP, LC/NC tools and how GenAI will support the developer. And we will explain how all this can work for customers who have a lot of legacy custom code in their system.

Speakers : Boris Gebhardt and Tim Back

Link to presentation: https://dam.sap.com/mac/u/a/rQqcHh5?rc=10&doi=SAP1132140

Link to validation tutorial: https://developers.sap.com/tutorials/devtoberfest2024-week3-abap-session-validation.html

entry.title: 🟢 Developing and Transforming your ABAP Custom Code for Clean Core in SAP S/4HANA
entry.content:
In this session we introduce the new guidelines for clean core ABAP development in the well-known 3-tier extensibility model in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition and on-premise and handle transformation options for existing custom code to ABAP Cloud including outlook of future generative AI support. You will also learn how ABAP Test Cockpit will support governance of your clean core developments.

Speaker : Olga Dolinskaja and Thomas Fiedler

Link to presentation: https://dam.sap.com/mac/u/a/7iTe3Un?rc=10&doi=SAP1132138

Link to validation tutorial: https://developers.sap.com/tutorials/devtoberfest2024-week3-abap-session-validation.html

ABAP custom code tools: https://pages.community.sap.com/topics/abap-testing-analysis

ABAP development: https://pages.community.sap.com/topics/abap

entry.title: 🟢 What Software Developers Should Know About Key User Extensibility
entry.content:
Key User Extensibility is a major pillar of the SAP S/4HANA Extensibility concept. It enables business experts to do last mile extensions by their own. But it also provides a lot of value for developers.
In this session, we provide an overview on the key user tools and will answer the question why developers should care for it. We provide examples for extensions that are realized as combination of key user and developer extensibility. An outlook on how generative AI support will change the creation of extensions will round off the session.

Speaker : Thomas Schneider

Link to the presentation: https://dam.sap.com/mac/u/a/h5ASWwK?rc=10&doi=SAP1129984

Link to the validation tutorial: https://developers.sap.com/tutorials/devtoberfest2024-week3-abap-session-validation.html

entry.title: Re: Task 3 - Enhancing CDS View Entities with Annotations
entry.content:
Hii,

  Thanks for the submission. For better readability you can filter out on the Status Text instead of the Status Code.

entry.title: Re: Task 3 - Enhancing CDS View Entities with Annotations
entry.content:
Hi,

  Thanks for the submission. Please search without a '*'.

entry.title: Re: Task 3 - Enhancing CDS View Entities with Annotations
entry.content:
Hi,

  Thanks for the submission. Could you please search without a '*' ?

entry.title: 🟢 Major Updates for Embedded Analytics in ABAP Cloud
entry.content:
ABAP Core Data Services (CDS) based embedded analytics provides easy-to-use analytical tooling and allows you to drill down, aggregate, and display data from your core solution modeled with ABAP CDS.
In this session, we present analytical data modeling basics in CDS as well as the new multi-dimensional analysis generator for building native, multi-dimensional SAP Fiori apps on top of the ABAP CDS-based analytical data model.

Speakers : Andreas Riehl and Olivia Baumann

Link to Presentation - https://dam.sap.com/mac/u/a/TNfHUv1?rc=10&doi=SAP1125031

Validation tutorial: https://developers.sap.com/tutorials/devtoberfest2024-week1-abap-session-validation.html

entry.title: 🟢 Boost your Coding Efficiency: Explore Joule’s ABAP Developer Capabilities
entry.content:
Discover Joule's ABAP developer capabilities. Hear about detailed use cases, the generative AI road map, and insights on how these advancements can boost your productivity as a developer in your daily tasks.

Speakers : Jasmin Gruschke and Sebastian Baskovich

Link to the presentation: https://dam.sap.com/mac/u/a/4JDk96J?rc=10&doi=SAP1129989

Link to the validation tutorial: https://developers.sap.com/tutorials/devtoberfest2024-week3-abap-session-validation.html

entry.title: Re: Task 4 - Implement Criticality feature and create an Object page
entry.content:
hi,

  Thanks for the submission. Could you please display the Status text instead of code for better readability?

entry.title: 🟢 Revolutionize SAP BTP Extensions with AWS SDK for ABAP to implement a Clean Core Strategy
entry.content:
Learn how to call AWS services directly from ABAP Cloud runtime, unlocking new possibilities for your SAP applications. Get insights how to leverage this integration to build scalable, cloud-native extensions, and embrace the clean core principle for your digital transformation. Witness a live demo showcasing the ease of calling AWS directly from the ABAP Cloud runtime, and understand how to integrate SAP BTP with AWS services for enhanced functionality.

Speakers : Adam Hill and Jon Friesen ( Amazon Web Services )

Link to the presentation: https://dam.sap.com/mac/u/a/n9b8QH2?rc=10&doi=SAP1135587

Link to Validation Tutorial : https://developers.sap.com/tutorials/devtoberfest2024-week4-abap-session-validation.html

entry.title: Re: 🟢 Data Integration in ABAP Cloud with SQL services and CDS External Entities
entry.content: The link to presentation is updated in the description here as well as in the Youtube description. Please check.

entry.title: Re: 🟢 Understand the Clean Core Extensibility Options for Cloud ERP
entry.content:
Information on the different key user apps/tools is available here: https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0f69f8fb28ac4bf48d2b57b9637e81fa/3ccb50e724b045508fea8b2cf1774b2b.html.

In addition, please refer the individual to the deep dive sessioon on key user extensibility in week 3: What Software Developers Should Know About Key User Extensibility . 

Hope this helps.

entry.title: 🇮🇳 SAP CodeJam ABAP: SAP BTP, ABAP Environment & RAP (Hyderabad, India)
entry.content:
REGISTRATION IS NOW CLOSED FOR THIS EVENT - We will send confirmation emails a couple of weeks before the event and will manage a waitlist, in case some of the RSVPs are unable to make it to the event.

This SAP CodeJam event is on the topic of the ABAP Cloud, and specifically introduces attendees to ABAP Cloud and the ABAP RESTful Application Programming Model. ABAP Cloud is the ABAP-based development model for S/4HANA Cloud, S/4HANA on-premise, and SAP BTP, ABAP environment.

Learn how to use proven and new technologies, such as Core Data Services (CDS) and behavior definition and implementation languages, to build SAP Fiori apps and OData services on any of the ABAP Cloud options. In this hands-on experience, developers will create their first ABAP applications using ABAP Cloud, expose and consume services via HTTP, and explore the ABAP RESTful Application Programming Model.

This is an in-person event only (not virtual) and is planned for Thursday 23rd January 2025 in Hyderabad, India from 9:00 - 16:00 IST. 

Location: Tekskills India Pvt. Ltd,  Survey # 1050, Plot # S - 2, 505 , 5th Floor, Manjeera Trinity, Corporate, JNTU Hi-tech City Road, KPHB 3rd Phase, Kukatpally, Hyderabad, Telangana 500072

The event is kindly being hosted by Tekskills India Pvt. Ltd, thank you! Please note that spaces are limited - we can only accommodate 35 attendees. If you wish to attend, please indicate that using the "I'm attending" facility on this page.

The instructors are Developer Advocates Sheena M K and Shilpa Shankar. 

There are essential prerequisites that must be completed by every attendee, before arriving at the event location. They are documented here: abap-exercises-codejam/prerequisites.md at main · SAP-samples/abap-exercises-codejam (github.com).

For more information on SAP CodeJam events, to ask questions, and to chat with your fellow attendees, head over to the SAP CodeJam group.

entry.title: Re: 🇮🇳 SAP CodeJam ABAP: SAP BTP, ABAP Environment & RAP (Hyderabad, India)
entry.content:
Hello Santhosh,

   We have included you in the waitlist and shall let you know in case of any drop outs.

Thanks & Regards

Sheena.

entry.title: SAP CodeJam ABAP: SAP BTP, ABAP Environment & RAP (Hyderabad, India)
entry.content:
Registration for this event is closed 

This SAP CodeJam event is on the topic of the ABAP Cloud, and specifically introduces attendees to ABAP Cloud and the ABAP RESTful Application Programming Model. ABAP Cloud is the ABAP-based development model for S/4HANA Cloud, S/4HANA on-premise, and SAP BTP, ABAP environment.

Learn how to use proven and new technologies, such as Core Data Services (CDS) and behavior definition and implementation languages, to build SAP Fiori apps and OData services on any of the ABAP Cloud options. In this hands-on experience, developers will create their first ABAP applications using ABAP Cloud, expose and consume services via HTTP, and explore the ABAP RESTful Application Programming Model.

This is an in-person event only (not virtual) and is planned for Wednesday 22nd January 2025 in Hyderabad, India from 9:00 - 16:00 IST. 

Location: Tata Consultancy Services Limited,  Blocks SDB1 & SDB2, Sy. No. 255(Part), Adibatla Village,
Ibrahimpatnam Mandal, Ranga Reddy District, Hyderabad - 501 510, Telangana.

The event is kindly being hosted by Tata Consultancy Services Limited, thank you! Please note that spaces are limited - we can only accommodate 35 attendees. If you wish to attend, please indicate that using the "I'm attending" facility on this page.

The instructors are Developer Advocates Sheena M K and Shilpa Shankar. 

There are essential prerequisites that must be completed by every attendee, before arriving at the event location. They are documented here: abap-exercises-codejam/prerequisites.md at main · SAP-samples/abap-exercises-codejam (github.com).

For more information on SAP CodeJam events, to ask questions, and to chat with your fellow attendees, head over to the SAP CodeJam group.

entry.title: Re: 🇮🇳 SAP CodeJam ABAP: SAP BTP, ABAP Environment & RAP (Hyderabad, India)
entry.content:
Hello Rohith,

   We have included you in the waitlist and shall let you know in case of any drop outs.

Thanks & Regards

Sheena.

entry.title: 🇮🇳 Getting started with Generative AI Hub on SAP AI Core
entry.content:
REGISTRATION IS NOW CLOSED FOR THIS EVENT - We will send confirmation emails to first 30 registered participants one week before the event. Please comment below in case you want to be considered in the waitlist. If there are any dropouts, we can consider including those in the waitlist.

This SAP CodeJam event is on the topic of Getting started with Generative AI Hub on SAP AI Core and Python. You do not need to have any background in the topic, but a lot of curiosity!

In this CodeJam you will learn how to use Generative AI Hub on SAP AI Core to implement a retrieval augmented generation (RAG) use case to improve the responses of large language models (LLMs) and reduce hallucinations. You will learn how to deploy an LLM and the orchestration service on SAP AI Core and query it via SAP AI Launchpad and the Python SDK. Furthermore, you will learn about the most important genAI concepts and create and use text chunks and embeddings to improve your RAG response.

This is a free in-person event and is planned for Wednesday, March 5th in Kolkata, India 🇮🇳 from 9:00 to 16:00 IST. The language of the event and the content will be in English. The instructor is Developer Advocate Nora von Thenen from SAP.

The event is kindly being hosted by Capgemini Technology Services India Ltd. , thank you! Please note that spaces are limited - we can only accommodate 30 attendees. 

Location : Capgemini Technology Services India Limited (Candor Tech Space), Candor Tech SpaceIT/ITES SEZ, Tower A3 -1st to 4th Floor, Tower A2 – 1st Floor to 2nd Floor, Tower A1 1st Floor to 3rd Floor, Action Area -1D, Block No -DH, Newtown, Rajarhat, Kolkata, West Bengal - 700156

Prerequisites: Any Chromium-based web browser, such as Google Chrome, Microsoft Edge, etc.

Visit the SAP CodeJam group for more information on SAP CodeJam events, to ask questions, and to chat with your fellow attendees.

entry.title: 🇮🇳 Getting started with Generative AI Hub on SAP AI Core
entry.content:
REGISTRATION IS NOW CLOSED FOR THIS EVENT - We will send confirmation emails to first 30 registered participants one week before the event. Please comment below in case you want to be considered in the waitlist. If there are any dropouts, we can consider including those in the waitlist.

This SAP CodeJam event is on the topic of Getting started with Generative AI Hub on SAP AI Core and Python. You do not need to have any background in the topic, but a lot of curiosity!

In this CodeJam you will learn how to use Generative AI Hub on SAP AI Core to implement a retrieval augmented generation (RAG) use case to improve the responses of large language models (LLMs) and reduce hallucinations. You will learn how to deploy an LLM and the orchestration service on SAP AI Core and query it via SAP AI Launchpad and the Python SDK. Furthermore, you will learn about the most important genAI concepts and create and use text chunks and embeddings to improve your RAG response.

This is a free in-person event and is planned for Friday, March 7th in Pune, India 🇮🇳 from 9:00 to 16:00 IST. The language of the event and the content will be in English. The instructor is Developer Advocate Nora von Thenen from SAP.

The event is kindly being hosted by Bloomberg Data Services (India) Private Ltd., thank you! Please note that spaces are limited - we can only accommodate 25 attendees. 

Location : Bloomberg Data Services (India) Private Ltd, SkyOne, S. No. 210, 5th Floor,Kalyani Nagar,                                    Village Yerwada, Pune, IN, 411 006

Prerequisites: Any Chromium-based web browser, such as Google Chrome, Microsoft Edge, etc.

Visit the SAP CodeJam group for more information on SAP CodeJam events, to ask questions, and to chat with your fellow attendees.

Thanks & Regards

Sheena.
